Exercise 4 IF Function: D/E Ratio Risk Analysis + COUNT Functions 4.1 Dataset 4.2  Download Excel File 4.3 Data Entry & Formulas 4.4 COUNT Functions Analysis 4.5 Chart Creation 4.6 Chart Formatting 4.7 Interpretation 4.8 Sorting 4.9 Advanced Filter 4.10 Final Output 4.11 Conclusion

Exercise 4 IF Function: D/E Ratio Risk Analysis + COUNT Functions Learning Outcomes Analyse company debt-equity data for risk assessment Apply logical formulas (IF, Nested IF) for risk classification Use statistical functions (COUNT, COUNTA, COUNTIF, COUNTIFS) Create charts for leverage comparison 4.1 Dataset Company Debt (Rs) Equity (Rs) D/E Ratio Risk Level Leverage Category Interest Coverage Investment Recommendation Company A 500 1000 0.5 Low Under-Leveraged 4.00 BUY Company B 1200 800 1.5 High Over-Leveraged 1.33 CAUTION Company C 300 1500 0.2 Low Under-Leveraged 10.00 BUY Company D 700 1000 0.7 Medium Under-Leveraged 2.86 BUY D/E Risk Classification: - < 0.5 → Low - 0.5 – 1.0 → Medium - 1.0 – 1.5 → High - > 1.5 → Very High 4.2  Download Excel File Download Debt-Equity Data Excel 4.3 Data Entry & Formulas 4.3.1 Structure in Excel Column Description A Company B–C Inputs (Debt, Equity) D D/E Ratio E Risk Level F Leverage Category G Interest Coverage H Investment Recommendation 4.3.2 Formulas Used Calculation Formula D/E Ratio =B3/C3 Risk Level =IF(D3<0.5,“Low”,IF(D3<=1,“Medium”,IF(D3<=1.5,“High”,“Very High”))) Leverage Category =IF(D3>1,“Over-Leveraged”,“Under-Leveraged”) 4.4 COUNT Functions Analysis Calculation Formula Count numeric D/E values =COUNT(D3:D6) Count non-blank companies =COUNTA(A3:A6) Companies with D/E > 1 =COUNTIF(D3:D6,“>1”) High Risk companies =COUNTIF(E3:E6,“High”) Empty cells =COUNTBLANK(D3:D6) 4.5 Chart Creation 4.5.1 Recommended Charts Clustered Column Chart → Compare Debt vs Equity Pie Chart → Risk distribution 4.5.2 Diagram flowchart TD A[Select Data] --> B[Insert Tab] B --> C[Choose Chart] 4.5.3 Insight Company B is over-leveraged (high risk) Majority companies are financially stable 4.6 Chart Formatting Title: Company Debt vs Equity Comparison X-axis: Company Y-axis: Amount (Rs) Legend: Debt, Equity 4.7 Interpretation Companies A, C, and D maintain healthy financial structures with lower debt levels, making them suitable for investment. Company B shows higher financial risk due to excessive leverage and lower interest coverage. 4.8 Sorting Example Sort by D/E Ratio (Descending) -> Identify highest risk Sort by Interest Coverage (Ascending) -> Identify weakest firms 4.9 Advanced Filter Criteria Risk Level Low OR Investment Recommendation BUY 4.10 Final Output Risk classification using IF formulas Statistical summary using COUNT functions Chart-based comparison Investment decision insights 4.11 Conclusion This experiment demonstrates how Excel logical and statistical functions can be used for financial risk evaluation, enabling automated and data-driven decision-making.
